text: Coinbase Reports $394 Million Net Loss as Q1 Revenue Falls 31% According to Bloomberg, Coinbase reported a net loss of approximately $394 million in the first quarter of 2026, compared with net income of $66 million a year earlier. Revenue fell 31% year-over-year to $1.41 billion, following a 20% decline in the previous quarter, as weaker crypto prices and lower trading activity weighed on results. Coinbase recently announced plans to cut about 14% of its workforce, with restructuring costs expected to reach up to $60 million. Bloomberg also noted that Morgan Stanley has launched lower-fee crypto trading on its E*Trade platform, potentially increasing competitive pressure on crypto exchanges. — link

text: The Memory Wall: Where AI's Second-Order Effects Hit Silicon The "memory wall" in AI stems from a physical separation between data and math, leading to a system where 60% of energy is wasted just moving information rather than processing it Compute-in-memory (CIM) aims to disrupt this by performing operations directly within memory cells, bypassing traditional architectural bottlenecks that legacy manufacturers are slow to abandon

text: Natural Language Autoencoders What if we could read an AI model's thoughts? Natural Language Autoencoders (NLAs) —> a new interpretability method that translates an AI's internal numerical activations into human-readable text to reveal its underlying "thoughts." This technology allows researchers to detect hidden motivations and evaluation awareness, such as when a model suspects it is being tested for safety, even when it does not explicitly say so in its output

text: South Korea to Begin Virtual Asset Taxation in January 2027 According to Edaily, the South Korean government plans to begin taxing virtual assets as scheduled starting in January next year. The National Tax Service is currently coordinating with the country's five major virtual asset operators — Upbit, Bithumb, Coinone, Korbit, and Gopax — to formulate detailed taxation measures. Under the current Income Tax Act, gains exceeding KRW 2.5 million will be subject to a total tax rate of 22% from January 1 next year, including 20% income tax and 2% local income tax. — link

text: BNY, the world's largest custodian bank, plans to offer digital asset custody services in Abu Dhabi through local partners Finstreet and ADI Foundation, while building regulated digital asset infrastructure anchored in Abu Dhabi Global Market (ADGM). The initiative will initially focus on BTC and ETH custody, with plans to later expand to stablecoins and tokenized assets. BNY oversees about $59 trillion in assets under custody and administration. — link

text: Santiment said Zcash has risen 75% over the past week but remains 22% below its $735 breakout level from six months ago. The firm said privacy coins are gaining retail attention amid weak government trust, surveillance concerns, tighter exchange rules and AI-driven data tracking. Santiment added that if stablecoin and KYC rules keep tightening, decentralized privacy networks could see renewed demand, while low market caps make many privacy coins high-upside momentum plays. — link

text: Highlight Clip Eric Trump: How Crypto Will Change the Future of Finance On November 14, 2025, Eric Trump, son of former President Trump, told Yahoo Finance Editor Brian Sozzi that cryptocurrencies will bring trillions of dollars into the U.S., with stablecoins facilitating the return of dollars. He revealed some Trump family-owned buildings are undergoing their first asset tokenization attempts. Additionally, he said Bitcoin addresses the shortcomings of physical gold, with people treating it as "digital gold," causing funds to flow from gold to Bitcoin. Finally, he criticized the outdated, long-monopolized banking system as unfriendly to ordinary users, emphasizing that cryptocurrencies are an inevitable trend and the future of finance. — link
